mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expansion signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity. Positive GDP growth momentum over the prior quarter produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as equity valuations re-rate upward in response to improved economic fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 value 11092.0 reflects ongoing economic activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP quarters produce negative SPX returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows. When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative as equity valuations compress in response to higher cost of capital.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day forward returns exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal labor market tightness. When UNRATE remains below 5.5% AND PAYEMS growth is positive month-over-month, labor cost inflation pressures build, constraining corporate profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC magnitude below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline signals weakening supply-side capacity and potential stagflation risk. A month-over-month contraction in INDPRO produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums upward in response to supply constraints.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO value 119.657 reflects recent production levels
failure_condition: When INDPRO contracts, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO change and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer demand momentum. When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, aggregate demand strength drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0 AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS 357013.0 and HOUST 2004.0 reflect demand activity
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the joint interaction effect has Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 3.5% signals monetary policy failure and real rate compression concerns. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days become negative as investors demand higher equity risk premiums to compensate for inflation erosion of real returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL value 194.1 reflects recent price levels
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between inflation rate and 60-day forward returns exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rise of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as equity markets immediately reprice downward in response to weakening employment conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE 5.0% provides baseline for deterioration measurement
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC between unemployment change and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth combined with declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals monetary accommodation supporting demand expansion. When GDPC1 growth is positive AND FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25pp month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days turn positive as the combination of growth and easing reduces equity risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 AND FEDFUNDS month-over-month decline > 0.25pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 11092.0 and FEDFUNDS 3.26% provide baseline for growth and rate signals
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
